reasoning: The baseline number of 'Explosions/Remote violence' events in Germany for the 360 days leading up to April 2026 appears to be zero (yielding a baseline value of 1.000, i.e., 1 + 0). Thus, to resolve YES, there must be more than 10 (i.e., 11 or more) such events in Germany in a single 30-day window leading up to the given resolution dates. This category includes military actions (artillery, airstrikes) or political terror attacks (IEDs, grenades) and excludes ordinary criminality like ATM bombings or unintentional UXO discoveries. Generating 11+ such events in a 30-day period would require an unprecedented campaign of terror or a descent into armed conflict—an exceedingly rare scenario for a stable democracy like Germany. Consequently, the probabilities remain at the minimum allowed for near-term dates and increase only slightly for the most distant horizons reflecting deep baseline uncertainty over a decade, but remaining very low.
